---
mode: 'agent'
description: 'Add full TSDoc + Swagger JSDoc documentation to an API route or repository file, following the repo documentation standard.'
tools: ['codebase', 'editFiles', 'search', 'usages']
---
# Add Thorough API Documentation

You are adding production-quality documentation to the OctoCAT Supply Chain Management API.

## TSDoc Standard (Repository Files)

Every **public method** in a repository class must have a TSDoc block with ALL of the following tags:

```typescript
/**
 * Brief one-line description of what the method does.
 *
 * @param db - The active better-sqlite3 Database instance
 * @param id - The numeric primary key of the record to retrieve
 * @returns The matching entity cast as the TypeScript model type
 * @throws {NotFoundError} When no record exists for the given id
 * @throws {DatabaseError} On unexpected SQLite errors
 * @example
 * const supplier = getSupplierById(db, 42);
 * console.log(supplier.supplierName);
 */
```

### Tags Reference
| Tag | Required? | Notes |
|-----|-----------|-------|
| `@param` | Yes — one per parameter | Include the param name and a brief description |
| `@returns` | Yes | Describe the shape/type returned |
| `@throws` | Yes — one per thrown error class | Use the actual class name from `src/utils/errors.ts` |
| `@example` | Yes — at least one realistic example | Show a call + what the caller does with the result |

## Swagger JSDoc Standard (Route Files)

Every route file must have a Swagger JSDoc block at the **top of the file** and an `@openapi` block per endpoint:

```typescript
/**
 * @openapi
 * /suppliers:
 *   get:
 *     summary: List all suppliers
 *     tags: [Suppliers]
 *     responses:
 *       200:
 *         description: Array of supplier objects
 *         content:
 *           application/json:
 *             schema:
 *               type: array
 *               items:
 *                 $ref: '#/components/schemas/Supplier'
 *       500:
 *         description: Internal server error
 */
```

### Swagger Requirements
- `summary` — one-line description
- `tags` — must match the existing tag groupings in `api-swagger.json`
- `requestBody` — required for `POST` and `PUT`; use `$ref` to existing schemas where they exist
- `responses` — document **all** possible status codes: `200`/`201`, `400`, `404`, `409`, `422`, `500`
- Parameters (`path`, `query`) must use `in: path` / `in: query` with `schema.type`

## Reference Files

Study these files as the authoritative style guide before writing any docs:
- `src/repositories/suppliersRepo.ts` — gold standard TSDoc
- `src/routes/order.ts` — gold standard Swagger JSDoc
- `api-swagger.json` — existing schema `$ref` targets

## What to Update

1. Add/update TSDoc on every public method in the target file
2. Add/update Swagger JSDoc on every route handler in the target file
3. If architectural behavior changed, append a note to `docs/backend-architecture.md`
4. Do **not** modify test files, migration files, or seed files

## Output Location & Naming

Write the documented version to a **new file** — do not modify the original source file:
```
api/generated/prompt-file-docs-thorough/{source-basename}.{YYYYMMDD-HHmmss}.documented.ts
```

- `{source-basename}` — name of the source file without extension (e.g. `suppliersRepo` from `suppliersRepo.ts`)
- `{YYYYMMDD-HHmmss}` — current date and time (e.g. `20260302-143000`)

Example: `api/generated/prompt-file-docs-thorough/suppliersRepo.20260302-143000.documented.ts`

Do **not** modify the original source file. Adjust any internal relative paths in the output copy as needed. As the **very first line** of the output file, add:

```typescript
// 🤖 Generated by: prompt-file-docs-thorough | {source-basename}.ts | {YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss}
```

## Validation Checklist (verify before finishing)

- [ ] Every `@param` has a name and description
- [ ] Every method has at least one `@throws` (or explicitly `@throws — never` if impossible)
- [ ] Every method has at least one `@example`
- [ ] Every route documents all possible response codes
- [ ] No `any` types introduced in JSDoc type annotations
